theme: jeweler's scale whose wobble encodes the law
title: The Jeweler's Wobbling Scale
strategies: trivial: pool all training readings into one global constant variance about the known mean mu, ignoring the load entirely -> reproduces the checker's own baseline construction (~0.1)

greedy: notice the wobble grows with load and fit ONE smooth power law A*x^p to the (load, empirical-variance) pairs by log-log least squares over ALL training data; tracks the training window passably but a single smooth shape cannot express the mechanism's regime switch, so it extrapolates on the wrong curvature to heavier held-out loads

strong: recognise the signal lives in the SECOND moment and that the mechanism is two regimes (elastic multiplicative power law, then additive wear-rate law) pinned together at their INTERSECTION; grid-search the knee load x0, fit a power law left of it and a continuity-constrained line right of it, and emit the exact piecewise expression via a Heaviside step switch so it generalises past the training window

headroom above strong: refine the knee search with finer granularity / robust (non-OLS) sub-fits and account for the heteroscedastic weighting of the per-load variance estimates themselves to push held-out NLL further toward the noise floor
